commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The Babylonians, and all the Chaldeans, Pekod, and Shoa, and Koa, and all the Assyrians with them: all of them desirable young men, captains and rulers, great lords and renowned, all of them riding upon horses. Protestants, "Pekod, and Shoah, and Roa: "(Haydock) which Junius takes to be the three great divisions of the empire. Others suppose they denote the dignities, though they occur not in Daniel. Hebrew, "Phachat, and Saganim, (ver. ) and Schalischim "officers mentioned Exodus xiv. , and 2 Kings xxiii. Renowned, called to the assemblies and councils of the prince, Numbers i. (Calmet) Horsemen. Protestants, "all of them riding upon horses.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
